message:
feat(rch): complete Drained status integration across all UI layers

CLI Commands (commands/mod.rs):
- Add "drained" → style.info("Drained") display mapping
- Update queue_status to count drained workers as offline
- Add drained indicator symbol (○) distinct from draining (◐)
- Update disabled symbol from ○ to ⊘ for visual distinction

Status Display (status_display.rs):
- Add drained status styling with info color
- Map "unhealthy" to "unreachable" for canonical naming
- Update test assertion to expect "unreachable" output

TUI State (state.rs):
- Add WorkerStatus::Drained and WorkerStatus::Disabled enum variants

TUI App (app.rs):
- Parse "drained" and "disabled" status strings from daemon responses
- Map to appropriate WorkerStatus variants

TUI Status (status.rs):
- Map Drained to StatusIndicator::Info
- Map Disabled to StatusIndicator::Pending
- Add worker symbols for drained (○) and disabled (⊘)

TUI Widgets (widgets.rs):
- Add render cases for Drained and Disabled in workers panel
- Use bullet_empty symbol with appropriate colors

Workers UI (ui/workers.rs):
- Add drained and disabled status icon rendering
- Group unreachable/offline/unhealthy under "Offline" display

Daemon API (api.rs):
- Include "drained" in status string output

Daemon Workers (workers.rs):
- Handle Drained status in worker management logic

This completes the Drained lifecycle status across all display surfaces,
enabling users to clearly see when workers have finished draining.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
